basicAuthUser.description: The username of the data source account

dataLinks.description: Add links to existing fields. Links will be shown in log row details next to the field value.

datasourceUid.description: The tracing data source the data link should navigate to. When set, the link becomes an internal Grafana link and the url field is treated as a query for that data source.

field.description: Can be exact field name or a regex pattern that will match on the field name.

urlDisplayLabel.description: Use to override the button label.

defaultQueryMode.description: Default query mode to use for the data source. Defaults to 'Metrics'.

httpHeaders.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request. Header names are stored in jsonData (httpHeaderName<N>); header values are write-only in secureJsonData (httpHeaderValue<N>).

index.description: Name of your Elasticsearch index. You can use a time pattern, such as YYYY.MM.DD, or a wildcard for the index name.

interval.description: If you're using a pattern for your index, select the type, or no pattern.

keepCookies.description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.

logLevelField.description: Configure the field that determines the level of each log message.

logMessageField.description: Configure the field to be used for log messages.

maxConcurrentShardRequests.description: Maximum number of concurrent shards a search request can hit per node. Defaults to 5.

serverName.description: A Servername is used to verify the hostname on the returned certificate

timeField.description: Name of your time field. Defaults to @timestamp.

timeInterval.description: A lower limit for the auto group by time interval. Recommended to be set to write frequency, for example 1m if your data is written every minute.

tlsAuth.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

tlsAuthWithCACert.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

tlsSkipVerify.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

url.description: Specify a complete HTTP URL (for example https://example.com:8080)

secureValues.description: The password of the data source account
The client certificate can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed
The client key can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

examples.description: The defaults a new datasource starts with: no authentication, timeField '@timestamp', maxConcurrentShardRequests 5, defaultQueryMode 'metrics'. Fill in root.url and a concrete jsonData.index to get a working datasource.

apiKey.description: Elasticsearch API key auth: the backend sends `Authorization: ApiKey <value>` on every request (elasticsearch.go:125-131). Set jsonData.apiKeyAuth=true and provide the base64-encoded key in secureJsonData.apiKey.

basicAuth.description: Authenticate with an Elasticsearch cluster that requires HTTP Basic. Both basicAuth and basicAuthUser live at the datasource root; only basicAuthPassword is secret.

legacyDatabaseFallback.description: Datasources created before the index moved to jsonData.index stored it in the root-level `database` field instead. The backend's LoadConfig / NewDatasource promotes settings.Database to jsonData.index when the latter is empty (elasticsearch.go:164-170); the editor's indexChangeHandler always clears database on save (ElasticDetails.tsx:162), so this shape only appears on datasources that have never been re-saved through the editor.

logsWithDataLinks.description: Log-oriented configuration with an explicit log message / log level field and two data link entries: one external URL and one internal Grafana link (datasourceUid) that treats jsonData.dataLinks[*].url as a query for the linked data source.

noAuth.description: A network-isolated Elasticsearch with no HTTP-level auth. Index uses a daily logstash-style time pattern.

oauthForward.description: Forward the signed-in user's upstream OAuth identity to Elasticsearch. The editor writes only jsonData.oauthPassThru; there is no accompanying secret.

sigV4.description: AWS SigV4-signed requests to an AWS-managed Elasticsearch or OpenSearch cluster. The sigV4* jsonData fields are contributed by @grafana/aws-sdk's SIGV4ConnectionConfig, and the backend forces the SigV4 service namespace to 'es' whenever the SDK builds a SigV4 transport (elasticsearch.go:120-123). Requires the Grafana instance to have sigV4AuthEnabled=true for the editor to expose the option.

tlsMutualAuth.description: Elasticsearch requiring mTLS. jsonData.tlsAuth=true triggers the SDK to build a client-authenticated transport using serverName plus the PEM-encoded client cert and key in secureJsonData.

secureJsonData.tlsClientKey:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

tlsSelfSignedCA.description: Elasticsearch behind a private CA. jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true tells the SDK to verify the server certificate against the PEM-encoded CA in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.
